description_long: This is a vintage-style fire extinguisher with a cylindrical red metal body and a large, conical black nozzle attached to a brass valve assembly at the top. The extinguisher features a worn, yellowed label with faded graphics and text, partially peeling off the front. The brass valve mechanism includes a round, flat control knob and a short, curved pipe leading to the nozzle, which is secured at an angle. A metal handle is attached near the top for carrying or operation, and the body shows signs of age, such as scratches and chipped paint. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible rivets and seams, and the extinguisher appears to be a pressurized type, likely intended for use in garages, workshops, or industrial urban settings. The object is robust and built primarily from metal, with a utilitarian finish and clear evidence of prior use.
description: A vintage red metal fire extinguisher with a large black conical nozzle, brass valve assembly, and a worn yellow label on its body. The extinguisher shows signs of age and use, with a utilitarian and robust design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the yellowed, partially torn label, the brass valve, and the large black nozzle extending to the left.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the plain red metal body, the handle, and the back side of the label with less visible detail.
description_view_2: Direct rear view displaying the cylindrical red body, the nozzle extending to the right, and no visible label.
description_view_3: Side view with the black nozzle facing forward, partially obscuring the body and label, and the valve assembly visible at the top.
attribute_label_or_signage: A large, yellowed, rectangular label with faded graphics and text is present on the front of the cylinder, partially torn and worn.
attribute_hose_or_nozzle: A large, conical black nozzle is attached to the brass valve assembly at the top, extending outward and angled to the side.
